title: Digestible Energy Measurement by Proximate Analysis in Mice
author: Trina Knotts
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.2vbge2n

steps.instruction: Mice are singly housed in clean cages with fresh food and water and low bedding.
Weigh mouse and “food-in” (grams of rodent diet provided at start of experiment).
Save several pellets of rodent diet for analysis (e.g., for energy or macronutrient content)-Store at 4C in airtight container.
At 24h, weigh mouse and “food –out” (grams of rodent diet remaining)
At the end of 48h collection period, weigh mouse and “food-out”.
Swap out the cage bottoms with new clean bottoms, replace feeder insert/water bottle and return animals to housing system.

For each cage, carefully inspect and remove each piece of bedding using tweezers to collect all feces. Inspect each pellet of feces to assure that there is no spilled food or fibers of hair or bedding attached. If significant food spillage is found in cage bottom, weigh in pre-tared weigh boat to add back to food measurement.
Place feces in labeled pre-weighed 1.5 ml tube.
Subtract this value from original tube weight to determine fecal “wet” weight.
After collection, fecal samples are stored at -80C until analysis for bomb calorimetry and proximate analysis.

48h food intake 48h fecal “wet” weight fecal energy density total fecal energy loss/day fecal energy loss as a percent of energy intake breakdown of fecal energy loss: protein, fat, carbohydrate Enclose fecal/diet sample log with samples and send on dry ice to: Mouse Biology Program C/O UC Davis MMPC University of California 2795 Second Street, Suite 400 Davis, CA 95618 530-757-3333 Please include MBP/MMPC project # in reference section on package documentation and send the FedEx/UPS tracking number information to: mmpc-bodyc@ucdavis.edu
